---
title: "Michael Kerr"
toc: false
about: 
    template: trestles
    image: profile.jpg
    links:
        -   icon: file-earmark-pdf
            text: CV
            href: cv.pdf
        -   icon: envelope
            text: michael.kerr@uga.edu
            href: mailto:michael.kerr@uga.edu
        -   icon: github
            text: Github
            href: https://github.com/mpk15956
        -   icon: linkedin
            text: LinkedIn
            href: https://www.linkedin.com/in/mpk15956
---

I am a computational geographer specializing in high-performance computing and machine learning for complex spatial systems. My current research is on spatial statistics, optimization, and algorithmic equity. I aim to identify robust, scale-invariant patterns in environmental data. As I transition to graduate research, my focus is shifting toward geospatial computer vision and scaling neural rendering models (NeRFs) for real-time edge hardware. I am also investigating how dynamical systems, PDEs, and tensor methods can be used to develop interpretable, physics-informed machine learning models. My goal is to translate advanced mathematical theory into practical, sustainable strategies.

## Research Interests
* **Computer Vision** & Edge Computing 
* **Spatial Optimization** & Algorithmic Fairness
* **Topological Data Analysis (TDA)** & Spatial Statistics
* **Scientific Machine Learning (SciML)** & Tensor Decomposition

## Education
**University of Georgia**, Athens, GA  
*B.S. in Geography & Applied Mathematics* (Expected July 2026)
